title: Node Test Runner, Runtime Faults, Compile Rejections, Async Leaks, and CLI Tests · Type Recall
summary: A delayed maintainer review reconstructs node test runner, runtime faults, compile rejections, async leaks, and cli tests from revision, runtime, command, unknown input, validation result, request identity, response fixture, promise state, cache decision, output, abort, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for layered runtime and compile-time evidence harness after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Separate pure validator and reducer, fetch, readline, cache, persistence, subprocess, compile-negative, fuzz-like, and controlled live smoke evidence.
Use node:test hooks, deterministic fixtures, scoped mocks, injected ports, fake clocks or deferred promises, and independent state.
Use original minimal unknown payloads that cover valid, changed, malformed, hostile, oversized, aborted, and reordered behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
